<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A of the Banking Regulation
Act, 1949, and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in
this regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest
so to do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text:
A. Short Title and Commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks -
Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
Provided that a bank's existing Information Technology (IT) outsourcing
agreements regardless of whether they are due for renewal on or after the
effective date of these Directions shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions either at the time of renewal or by April 10, 2026, whichever is earlier.
However, the bank's new IT outsourcing agreements that come into force on or
after the effective date of these Directions, shall comply with the provisions of
these Directions from the date of agreement itself.
Provided further that nothing in the preceding proviso shall be construed as
permitting non-compliance with any other extant regulatory instructions or statutory
requirements applicable to such arrangements.

children.title: B. Applicability and Scope
children.text:
B. Applicability and Scope
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to Small Finance Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
4.
The scope of provisions contained in Chapter, III shall be as under:
(1) The provisions shall apply to outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank
with a service provider which may be either a member of the group /
conglomerate to which the bank belongs, or an unrelated party, which is
located in India or elsewhere, for outsourcing of financial services like
applications processing (loan origination, credit card), document processing,

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
marketing and research, supervision of loans, data processing and back office
related activities.
Provided that for outsourced services relating to credit cards, the provisions
set out in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Credit Cards and
Debit Cards: Issuance and Conduct) Directions, 2025, as amended from time
to time, shall also apply to the applicable entities.
(2) The provisions shall also apply, mutatis mutandis, to subcontracted activities.
For this purpose, the outsourcing contract shall provide for prior approval or
consent of the bank before a service provider engages any subcontractor for
all or part of the outsourced activity. Before granting such consent, the bank
shall review the subcontracting arrangement and ensure that the same
complies with the Directions set out in Chapter III of these Directions.
(3) The provisions shall not apply to outsourcing of:

children.title: (i) IT services as defined in paragraph 53(1) of these Directions, unless
children.text:
(i) IT services as defined in paragraph 53(1) of these Directions, unless
specified otherwise in respective paragraphs of Chapter IV;

children.title: (ii) activities unrelated to banking services like usage of courier, catering of
children.text:
(ii) activities unrelated to banking services like usage of courier, catering of
staff, housekeeping and janitorial services, security of the premises, and
movement and archiving of records; and

children.title: (iii) audit-related assignments to Chartered Accountant firms which shall
children.text:
(iii) audit-related assignments to Chartered Accountant firms which shall
continue to be governed by the instructions and guidelines issued by the
Department of Supervision, RBI.

children.title: 5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:
children.text:
5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:
(1) The provisions shall apply to a bank's material outsourcing of IT services, as
defined in paragraph 53(2). In this context, 'Outsourcing of IT Services' shall
include outsourcing of the following services:

children.title: (i) IT infrastructure management, maintenance and support (hardware,
children.text:
(i) IT infrastructure management, maintenance and support (hardware,
software or firmware);

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) network and security solutions, and maintenance (hardware, software or
children.text:
(ii) network and security solutions, and maintenance (hardware, software or
firmware);

children.title: (iii) application development, maintenance, and testing by Application Service
children.text:
(iii) application development, maintenance, and testing by Application Service
Providers (ASPs) including ATM Switch ASPs;

children.title: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;
children.text: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;

children.title: (v) cloud computing services;
children.text: (v) cloud computing services;

children.title: (vi) managed security services; and
children.text: (vi) managed security services; and

children.title: (vii) management of IT infrastructure and technology services associated with
children.text:
(vii) management of IT infrastructure and technology services associated with
payment system ecosystem.
(2) The provisions shall not apply to the following services:

children.title: (i) corporate internet banking services obtained by a bank as a corporate
children.text:
(i) corporate internet banking services obtained by a bank as a corporate
customer or sub-member of another Regulated Entity (RE);
Provided that for the purpose of these Directions, the following indicative
(but not exhaustive) list of entities shall be considered as REs - Commercial
Banks; Local Area Banks; other Small Finance Banks; Payments Banks;
Regional Rural Banks; Non-Banking Financial Companies in Base Layer
(NBFC - BL), Middle Layer (NBFC - ML), and Upper Layer (NBFC - UL); All
India Financial Institutions; Credit Information Companies; Urban Co-
operative Banks; Rural Co-operative Banks; and, Payment System
Operators.
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Commercial Banks' means banking
companies (other than Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, Payments
Banks and Regional Rural Banks), corresponding new banks, and the State
Bank of India as defined respectively under clauses (c), (da), and (nc) of
section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

children.title: (ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) / Penetration
children.text:
(ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) / Penetration
Testing (PT), Information Systems Audit, and security review;

children.title: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);
children.text: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iv) procurement of IT hardware or appliances;
children.text: (iv) procurement of IT hardware or appliances;

children.title: (v) acquisition of IT software, product or application (e.g., Core Banking
children.text:
(v) acquisition of IT software, product or application (e.g., Core Banking
Solution (CBS), database, and security solutions) on a licence or
subscription basis, and any enhancements made to such licensed third-
party applications by the vendor (as upgrades) or on specific change
request made by a bank;

children.title: (vi) any maintenance service (including security patches, and bug fixes) for IT
children.text:
(vi) any maintenance service (including security patches, and bug fixes) for IT
infrastructure or licensed products, provided by the Original Equipment
Manufacturer (OEM) themselves, in order to ensure continued usage of the
same by the bank;

children.title: (vii) applications provided by financial sector regulators or institutions such as
(viii) platforms provided by entities such as Reuters, Bloomberg, and Society for
children.text:
(viii) platforms provided by entities such as Reuters, Bloomberg, and Society for
Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT);

children.title: (ix) any other off-the-shelf products (e.g., anti-virus software, and email
(x) services obtained by a bank as a sub-member of a Centralised Payment
children.text:
(x) services obtained by a bank as a sub-member of a Centralised Payment
System (CPS) from another RE; and

children.title: (xi) Business Correspondent (BC) services, payroll processing, and statement
children.text:
(xi) Business Correspondent (BC) services, payroll processing, and statement
printing.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text: C. Definitions

children.title: 6. In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
children.text:
6. In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
(1) 'Outsourcing' means use of a third party (either an affiliated entity within a
corporate group or an entity that is external to the corporate group) by a bank to
perform activities on a continuing basis that would normally be undertaken by the
bank itself, now or in the future. 'Continuing basis' shall include agreements for a

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
limited period.
(2) 'Group' shall be as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks -
Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time,
for the purpose of intragroup transactions and exposures;

children.title: 7. Some other terms pertaining to outsourcing of financial services and IT services
children.text:
7. Some other terms pertaining to outsourcing of financial services and IT services
have been defined in their respective Chapters as per their applicability.

children.title: 8. All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
children.text:
8. All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or the Reserve
Bank of India Act, 1934, or the Information Technology Act, 2000, or the Companies
Act, 2013, and Rules made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-
enactment thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial
parlance, as the case may be.

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of the Board
hierarchy.text: Chapter II - Role of the Board

children.title: 9. The outsourcing of any activity by a bank does not diminish its obligations, and those
children.text:
9. The outsourcing of any activity by a bank does not diminish its obligations, and those
of its Board and Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility for the
outsourced activity.

children.title: A. Board-Approved Policy
children.text: A. Board-Approved Policy

children.title: 10. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in place
children.text:
10. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in place
corresponding comprehensive Board-approved outsourcing policy, the coverage of
which is indicated in paragraph 20 and paragraph 57, respectively.

children.title: B. Key Responsibilities
children.text: B. Key Responsibilities

children.title: 11. The Board or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, as
children.text:
11. The Board or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, as
applicable for financial or IT outsourcing, shall be responsible for putting in place a
framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective
outsourcing arrangements, laying down appropriate approval authorities
depending on risks and materiality, and undertaking regular review.

children.title: 12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services,
children.text:
12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services,
(1) the Board, or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been
delegated, shall be responsible, inter alia, for:

children.title: (i) approving a framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending
children.text:
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (iii) undertaking regular review of outsourcing strategies and arrangements
children.text:
(iii) undertaking regular review of outsourcing strategies and arrangements
for their continued relevance, and safety and soundness;

children.title: (iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced,
children.text:
(iv) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced,
and approving such arrangements;

children.title: (v) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half-yearly basis; and
children.text: (v) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half-yearly basis; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the
children.text:
(vi) ensuring submission of an Annual Compliance Certificate giving the
particulars of contracts for outsourcing of financial services, the
prescribed periodicity of audit by internal or external auditor, major
findings of the audit, and action taken to the Department of Supervision,
RBI.
(2) the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) of a bank shall:

children.title: (i) monitor the system of internal audit of all outsourced activities; and
children.text: (i) monitor the system of internal audit of all outsourced activities; and

children.title: (ii) review the ageing analysis of entries pending reconciliation with
children.text:
(ii) review the ageing analysis of entries pending reconciliation with
outsourced vendors and make efforts to reduce the old outstanding
items therein at the earliest.

children.title: 13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
children.text:
13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
for:

children.title: (i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
children.text:
(i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
children.text:
(ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
prospective outsourcing arrangements;

children.title: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;
children.text: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;

children.title: (iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of
children.text:
(iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of
interest arising out of third-party engagements, especially when permitting an
exception to the requirement that the service provider of outsourced services,
if not a group company, shall not be owned or controlled by any director, key
managerial personnel, approver of the outsourcing arrangement, or their
relatives as indicated under the proviso to paragraph 54 of these Directions;
and

children.title: (v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior
children.text:
(v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior
Management on the monitoring and control activities.

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services

children.title: A. Definitions
children.text: A. Definitions

children.title: 14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
children.text:
14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
means arrangements, which if disrupted, have the potential to significantly impact
the business operations, reputation or profitability of a bank. 'Materiality' of
outsourcing shall be based on the:

children.title: (i) level of importance to the bank of the activity being outsourced;
children.text: (i) level of importance to the bank of the activity being outsourced;

children.title: (ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the bank on various parameters such
children.text:
(ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the bank on various parameters such
as earnings, solvency, liquidity, funding, capital, and risk profile;

children.title: (iii) likely impact on the bank's reputation and brand value, and ability to achieve
(iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the bank;
children.text:
(iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the bank;
and

children.title: (v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
children.text:
(v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
bank outsources various functions to the same service provider.

children.title: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced
children.text: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced

children.title: 15. A bank which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
children.text:
15. A bank which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not outsource
core management functions including Internal Audit, compliance function, and
decision-making functions like determining compliance with KYC norms for
opening deposit accounts, giving sanction for loans (including retail loans), and
management of investment portfolio.

children.title: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 16. A bank which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
children.text:
16. A bank which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
approval from RBI whether the service provider is located in or outside India.

children.title: 17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by a bank shall not
children.text:
17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by a bank shall not
diminish its obligations including to its customers and RBI, and those of its Board
and Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility for the outsourced

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
activity. The bank shall, therefore, be responsible for the actions of its service
provider including Direct Sales Agents (DSAs) / Direct Marketing Agents (DMAs)
and recovery agents, and the confidentiality of information pertaining to the
customers that is available with the service provider. The bank shall retain ultimate
control of the outsourced activity.

children.title: 18. A bank shall ensure that:
children.text: 18. A bank shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines and conditions of approval,
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
children.text:
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
not impede RBI in carrying out its supervisory functions and objectives, or
diminish the ability of a bank to fulfil its obligations to the regulator /
supervisor;

children.title: (iii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of a bank's
children.text:
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of a bank's
internal control, business conduct, or reputation;

children.title: (v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing
children.text:
(v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing
the services as would be employed by the bank, if the activities were
conducted within the bank and not outsourced; and

children.title: (vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer
children.text:
(vi) the service provider shall not be owned or controlled by any director or officer
/ employee of the bank or their relatives having the same meaning as
assigned under Companies Act, 2013 and the Rules framed thereunder, as
amended from time to time.

children.title: 19. A bank shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs)
children.text:
19. A bank shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs)
and Suspicious Transactions Reports (STRs) to FIU or any other competent
authority in respect of its customer related activities carried out by the service
providers.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: D. Governance Framework
children.text:
D. Governance Framework
D.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 20. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
children.text:
20. A bank intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
comprehensive outsourcing policy, approved by its Board, which shall incorporate,
inter alia, the following:

children.title: (i) criteria for selection of such activities, and service providers;
children.text: (i) criteria for selection of such activities, and service providers;

children.title: (ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(ii) parameters for defining 'material outsourcing' based on the broad criteria
indicated in paragraph 14 of these Directions;

children.title: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks, and materiality; and
children.text: (iii) delegation of authority depending on risks, and materiality; and

children.title: (iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
children.text:
(iv) systems to monitor and review these activities.
D.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 21. The Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible for:
children.text: 21. The Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible for:

children.title: (i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective
(ii) developing and implementing sound and prudent outsourcing policies and
(iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;
children.text: (iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;

children.title: (iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
children.text:
(iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
Board in a timely manner;

children.title: (v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
children.text:
(v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
scenarios, are in place and tested;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
policies; and

children.title: (vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
children.text:
(vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
material outsourcing risks as they arise.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: E. Risk Management
children.text:
E. Risk Management
E.1 Evaluation of the Risks

children.title: 22. A bank shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
children.text:
22. A bank shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering into
outsourcing arrangements:

children.title: (i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on
children.text:
(i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on
its own behalf, inconsistent with the overall strategic goals of the bank.

children.title: (ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
children.text:
(ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
or its customer interactions are inconsistent with the overall standards of the
bank.

children.title: (iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
children.text:
(iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
consumer, and prudential laws are not adequately complied with.

children.title: (iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error,
children.text:
(iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error,
or inadequate financial capacity of the service provider to fulfil obligations or
to provide remedies.

children.title: (v) Legal Risk - where a bank is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
children.text:
(v) Legal Risk - where a bank is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
punitive damages resulting from supervisory actions, or private settlements
due to omissions and commissions by the service provider.

children.title: (vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when a bank becomes over reliant on one
children.text:
(vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when a bank becomes over reliant on one
service provider, loses relevant internal skills preventing it from bringing the
activity back in-house, or enters into contracts that make speedy exits
prohibitively expensive.

children.title: (vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
children.text:
(vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
inappropriate underwriting or credit assessments.

children.title: (viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
children.text:
(viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
in the outsourcing arrangement.

children.title: (ix) Contractual Risk - where the bank may not have the ability to enforce the
children.text:
(ix) Contractual Risk - where the bank may not have the ability to enforce the
contract with the service provider.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of a
children.text:
(x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of a
bank over a service provider, more so when overall banking industry has
considerable exposure to one service provider.
E.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
children.text:
23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
customer information in the custody or possession of the service provider.

children.title: 24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a
children.text:
24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a
'need to know' basis, i.e., limited to those areas where the information is required
in order to perform the outsourced function.

children.title: 25. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
children.text:
25. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
service providers on a regular basis and require the service provider to disclose
security breaches.

children.title: 26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
children.text:
26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
entities, the bank shall take care to build strong safeguards so that there is no
comingling or combining of information, documents, records, and assets.

children.title: 27. A bank shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify the
children.text:
27. A bank shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify the
bank's customer information, documents, records, and assets to protect the
confidentiality of the information.

children.title: 28. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage
children.text:
28. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage
of confidential customer related information. In these eventualities, the bank shall
be liable to its customers for any damage.

children.title: F. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
F. Outsourcing Process
F.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 29. A bank shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
children.text:
29. A bank shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
outsourcing arrangement, to assess the capability of the service provider to
comply with obligations in the outsourcing agreement on an ongoing basis.

children.title: 30. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
children.text:
30. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
information, as applicable, about the service provider, including but not limited to:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;
children.text: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;

children.title: (ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
children.text:
(ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
provider, or a limited number of service providers;

children.title: (iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support
children.text:
(iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support
the proposed activity over the contracted period;

children.title: (iv) financial soundness, and ability to service commitments even under adverse
children.text:
(iv) financial soundness, and ability to service commitments even under adverse
conditions;

children.title: (v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints, and outstanding or
children.text:
(v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints, and outstanding or
potential litigation;

children.title: (vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
children.text:
(vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
and sub-contractors;

children.title: (vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting, and monitoring
children.text:
(vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting, and monitoring
procedures, and business continuity management; and

children.title: (viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
children.text:
(viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
jurisdiction in which the service provider operates and other events that may
impact data security and service performance.

children.title: 31. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
children.text:
31. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
the service provider to supplement the findings of its own due diligence.

children.title: 32. A bank shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
children.text:
32. A bank shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
compatible with those of the bank, and the acceptability of their standards of
performance including in the area of customer service.
F.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 33. A bank shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
children.text:
33. A bank shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
arrangement are carefully defined in written agreements and vetted by the bank's
legal counsel on their legal effect and enforceability. The agreement shall
appropriately reckon the associated risks and the strategies for mitigating or
managing them. The bank shall ensure that such an agreement is sufficiently
flexible to allow it to retain an appropriate level of control over the outsourcing, and

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
the right to intervene with appropriate measures to meet legal and regulatory
obligations. The agreement shall also bring out the nature of legal relationship
between the parties, i.e., whether agent-principal or otherwise.

children.title: 34. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:
children.text: 34. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:

children.title: (i) details of the activity being outsourced, including appropriate service, and
children.text:
(i) details of the activity being outsourced, including appropriate service, and
performance standards;

children.title: (ii) bank's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the
children.text:
(ii) bank's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the
outsourced activity available with the service provider;

children.title: (iii) regular and continuous monitoring, and assessment by the bank of the
(iv) prior approval or consent by the bank for the use of subcontractors by the
(v) controls for maintaining confidentiality of data including of its customers, and
(vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;
children.text: (vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;

children.title: (vii) bank's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
children.text:
(vii) bank's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
or external auditors, or by agents appointed to act on its behalf and to obtain
copies of any audit or review reports and findings made on the service
provider in conjunction with the services performed for the bank;

children.title: (viii) right of RBI or persons authorised by it to access the bank's documents,
(ix) right of RBI to cause an inspection to be made of the service provider by one
children.text:
(ix) right of RBI to cause an inspection to be made of the service provider by one
or more of its officers, employees or other authorised persons;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination, if
children.text:
(x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination, if
deemed necessary;

children.title: (xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
children.text:
(xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
even after the contract expires or gets terminated; and

children.title: (xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
children.text:
(xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
in accordance with legal and regulatory obligations of the bank.
F.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities
35.
A bank shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced activities and shall ensure that outsourcing agreements with its
service provider contain provisions to address the same.

children.title: 36. A bank shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
children.text:
36. A bank shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
services for review by its Board and Senior Management. The records shall be
updated promptly, and half-yearly reviews shall be placed before the Board.

children.title: 37. Regular audits, by either the internal auditors or external auditors of a bank, shall
children.text:
37. Regular audits, by either the internal auditors or external auditors of a bank, shall
assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in overseeing and
managing the outsourcing arrangement, the bank's compliance with its risk
management framework, and the requirements of these Directions.

children.title: 38. A bank shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
children.text:
38. A bank shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
condition of the service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its
outsourcing obligations. Such due diligence reviews, which shall be based on all
available information about the service provider, shall highlight any deterioration or
breach in performance standards, confidentiality, and security, and in operational
resilience or business continuity preparedness.

children.title: 39. Certain services, viz., outsourcing of cash management, might involve
children.text:
39. Certain services, viz., outsourcing of cash management, might involve
reconciliation of transaction between a bank and the service provider (or its
subcontractors). In such cases, the bank shall ensure that reconciliation of
transactions between itself and the service provider (or its subcontractors) is
carried out in a timely manner. A bank shall ensure that the reconciliation is carried
out as advised in RBI guidelines on 'Outsourcing of Cash Management -

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Reconciliation of Transactions' dated May 14, 2019, as amended from time to
time.
F.4 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan
40.
A bank shall require its service provider to develop and establish a robust
framework for documenting, maintaining, and testing business continuity and
recovery procedures. The bank shall ensure that the service provider
periodically tests the Business Continuity and Recovery Plan, and may also
consider occasional joint testing and recovery exercises with its service provider.

children.title: 41. In establishing a viable contingency plan, a bank shall consider the availability of
children.text:
41. In establishing a viable contingency plan, a bank shall consider the availability of
alternative service providers or the possibility of bringing the outsourced activity
back in-house in an emergency, and the costs, time, and resources that would be
involved.

children.title: 42. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
children.text:
42. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
information, documents, and records, and other assets so that in adverse
conditions or termination of the agreement, all documents, records of transactions
and information with the service provider, and assets of the bank, can be removed
from the possession of the service provider (in order to continue its business
operations), or deleted, destroyed, or rendered unusable.

children.title: 43. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing
children.text:
43. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing
agreement, or insolvency or liquidation of its service provider, a bank shall retain
an appropriate level of control over its outsourcing arrangement along with the
right to intervene with appropriate measures to continue its business operations
without incurring prohibitive expenses and disruption in the operations of the bank
and its services to the customers.
F.5 Termination

children.title: 44. If the services of a service provider are terminated by a bank, then it shall:
children.text: 44. If the services of a service provider are terminated by a bank, then it shall:

children.title: (i) publicise the same so as to ensure that its customers do not continue to deal
children.text:
(i) publicise the same so as to ensure that its customers do not continue to deal
with the service provider; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to
children.text:
(ii) inform Indian Banks' Association (IBA) of the reasons for termination to
enable IBA to maintain a caution list of such service providers for sharing
among banks.

children.title: G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
G.1 Outsourcing within a Group / Conglomerate

children.title: 45. (1) In a group structure, a bank may have back-office and service arrangements or
children.text:
45. (1) In a group structure, a bank may have back-office and service arrangements or
agreements with group entities such as sharing of premises, legal and other
professional services, hardware and software applications and centralized back-
office functions; outsourcing certain financial services to other group entities. In
principle, the risk management practices to be adopted by a bank while
outsourcing to a related party (i.e., party within the group / conglomerate) shall be
identical to those for a non-related party.
(2) While entering into such arrangements, a bank shall ensure that these:

children.title: (i) are appropriately documented in written agreements with details like scope of
(ii) do not lead to any confusion to the customers on whose products / services
children.text:
(ii) do not lead to any confusion to the customers on whose products / services
they are availing;

children.title: (iii) do not compromise the ability of the bank to identify and manage risk on a
children.text:
(iii) do not compromise the ability of the bank to identify and manage risk on a
stand-alone basis;

children.title: (iv) do not impinge on safety and soundness of the bank a stand-alone entity;
children.text: (iv) do not impinge on safety and soundness of the bank a stand-alone entity;

children.title: (v) do not prevent RBI from being able to obtain information required for the
children.text:
(v) do not prevent RBI from being able to obtain information required for the
supervision of the bank or pertaining to the group as a whole; and

children.title: (vi) incorporate a clause under the written agreements that there is a clear
children.text:
(vi) incorporate a clause under the written agreements that there is a clear
obligation for any service provider to comply with directions given by RBI in
relation to the activities of the bank.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) A bank shall ensure that its ability to carry out its operations in a sound fashion
would not be affected, if premises or other services (such as IT systems and
support staff) provided by the group entities become unavailable.
(4) If the premises of a bank are shared with the group entities for the purpose of
cross-selling, the bank shall take measures to ensure that the entity's
identification is distinctly visible and clear to the customers. The marketing
brochure used by the group entity and verbal communication by its staff / agent
in the bank's premises shall mention nature of arrangement of the entity with the
bank so that the customers are clear on the seller of the product.
(5) A bank shall not publish any advertisement or enter into any agreement stating
or suggesting or giving tacit impression that it is in any way responsible for the
obligations of its group entities.
G.2 Offshore outsourcing

children.title: 46. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
46. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that uphold confidentiality agreements and clauses.

children.title: 47. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:
children.text: 47. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and political, social, economic, and legal conditions, both
during the risk assessment and on a continuous basis, and establish sound
procedures for dealing with country risk. This includes having appropriate
contingency and exit strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that the availability of records to the bank and RBI will not be affected
children.text:
(iii) ensure that the availability of records to the bank and RBI will not be affected
even in the case of liquidation of the service provider or offshore custodian;

children.title: (iv) ensure activities outsourced outside India are conducted in a manner so as
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) ensure that, where the offshore service provider is a regulated entity, the
(vi) ensure that the regulatory authority of the offshore location does not have
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
children.text:
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
maintained does not extend to the operations of the bank in India on the
strength of the fact that the data is being processed there even though the
actual transactions are undertaken in India; and

children.title: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.
children.text: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.

children.title: 48. The overseas operations of a bank shall be governed by both, these Directions
children.text:
48. The overseas operations of a bank shall be governed by both, these Directions
and the host country guidelines, and in case there are differences, the more
stringent of the two shall prevail. However, where there is any conflict, the host
country guidelines shall prevail.

children.title: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
children.text: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services

children.title: 49. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
children.text:
49. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
customers against the bank, including the ability of the customers to obtain
redressal as applicable under relevant laws.

children.title: 50. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the
children.text:
50. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the
process of dealing with a bank, the bank shall incorporate a clause in the
corresponding product literature, and brochures, stating that services of the
service provider, including in sales and marketing of the products, may be used.
The role of the service provider may be indicated in broad terms.

children.title: 51. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
children.text:
51. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
compromised in any manner on account of outsourcing, i.e., responsibility for
redressal of customers' grievances related to outsourced services shall rest with
the bank. In case of microfinance loans, a declaration that, (i) the bank shall be
accountable for inappropriate behaviour of the employees of the service provider

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
and (ii) shall provide timely grievance redressal, shall be made in the loan
agreement, and in Fair Practices Code (FPC) displayed in its office, branch
premises, and its website.

children.title: 52. In addition to the above,
children.text:
52. In addition to the above,
(1) a bank shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery in accordance with
the provisions of Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Responsible
Business Conduct) Directions, 2025, and give wide publicity about it within
the bank through electronic and print media;
(2) the name and contact number of designated grievance redressal officer of the
bank shall be made known and widely publicised. The designated officer shall
ensure that genuine grievances of customers are redressed promptly without
involving delay. It shall be clearly indicated that bank's Grievance Redressal
Machinery will also deal with the issue relating to services provided by the
service provider; and
(3) the time frame fixed for responding to the complaints (maximum 30 days)
shall be placed on the bank's website. If a complaint is rejected wholly or
partly by a bank, and the complainant is not satisfied with the reply, or does
not get any reply within 30 days after the bank received the complaint, the
complainant shall have the option to seek grievance redressal under the
RBI's Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021.
